channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Tesla Expands into Saudi Arabia Amid Growing Demand for Electric Vehicles
item.description: Tesla starts selling cars in Saudi Arabia, a country with ambitious plans to adopt electric vehicles despite limited charging infrastructure and summer heat that drains EV batteries.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Former Facebook executive tells Senate committee company undermined US national security with China
item.description: Sarah Wynn-Williams, a former executive at Meta and author of an explosive insider account of her time at the social media giant testified before the Senate Judiciary Committee Tuesday, accusing the company of undermining national security and briefing China on U.S.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: No new hires, unless you prove AI can’t do the job: Shopify CEO
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, WGN 9

item.title: How cloud adoption solves some of IT’s biggest headaches
item.description: In an era when artificial intelligence (AI) and other resource-intensive technologies demand unprecedented computing power, data centers are starting to buckle, and CIOs are feeling the budget pressure.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Canon reportedly U-turns on price drops for two of its most popular cameras – and it could be the tip of the tariffs iceberg
item.description: Canon has reportedly canceled some planned price cuts for two of its most affordable cameras. Here's why it could be just the start of an expensive year for photographers.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Hackers go after influencers and content creators to hit followers with malware, steal data
item.description: Large social media channels are being targeted and abused in crypto scams, malware attacks, and more.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Shopify CEO: No new hires, unless you prove AI can't do the job
item.description: "Teams must demonstrate why they cannot get what they want done using AI," Shopify CEO Tobi Lutke wrote in a memo to employees.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kooiman, The Hill

item.title: Big San Jose industrial complex where hundreds could work pushes ahead
item.description: A big industrial complex in north San Jose that could accommodate hundreds of jobs has been approved.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: UALink releases inaugural GPU interconnect specification
item.description: The multivendor Ultra Accelerator Link (UALink) consortium has published its first specification aimed at delivering an open standard interconnect for AI clusters – and providing an alternative to Nvidia’s proprietary NVLink.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Don't be so quick to pre-order that Hori Piranha Plant Camera for Nintendo Switch 2 as there's a pretty big catch
item.description: Hori's Nintendo Switch 2 GameChat camera may be cheaper than the official option, but its resolution will be worse.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: From training dogs to intelligent machines: Here’s how reinforcement learning is teaching AI
item.description: Understanding intelligence and creating intelligent machines are grand scientific challenges of our times. The ability to learn from experience is a cornerstone of intelligence for machines and living beings alike.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: Trump administration disbands crypto regulations enforcement unit
item.description: The Trump administration disbanded the National Cryptocurrency Enforcement Team (NCET) according to a Monday memo issued by the Department of Justice (DOJ) outlining their efforts to remove regulatory framework for the growing digital finance sector.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: IRS acting commissioner is resigning over deal to send immigrants' tax data to ICE, AP sources say
item.description: The Internal Revenue Service’s acting commissioner is resigning over a deal to share immigrants’ tax data with Immigration and Customs Enforcement for the purpose of identifying and deporting people illegally in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Meta is bringing stricter parental controls to Facebook and Messenger
item.description: Meta is bringing its Teen Accounts, which have stricter parental controls, to its Facebook and Messenger platforms on Tuesday, expanding its teen service from just Instagram.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: How ChatGPT is helping bend websites to my will
item.description: I’m a writer, not a programmer, so until recently a lot of the hype around ChatGPT’s abitilies as a coding tool went over my head. But then I realized generative AI’s programming powers can be helpful for more than just coders. It can also help anyone else dabble in code to get things done.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Prague Zoo joins the effort to ensure the survival of a rare insect once considered extinct
item.description: The Prague Zoo has joined an international effort to ensure the survival of a rare insect that had been considered extinct for more than 80 years.
item.dc:creator: Karel Janicek, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How Apple thinks about the 6G evolution
item.description: With its C1 5G chip and huge networking tech development division, Apple is in the networking business. So, the company is heavily enmeshed in the development of the next-generation 6G networking standard.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Visual Studio Code stabilizes agent mode
item.description: Visual Studio Code 1.99, the latest release of Microsoft’s popular code editor, is now available. Highlights of the update center on GitHub Copilot agent mode, Next Edit Suggestions, and Copilot chat. Developers can access Visual Studio Code 1.99, also known as the March 2025 release, through code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Shopify CEO Tobi Lütke: AI is now a ‘fundamental expectation’ for employees
item.description: Shopify CEO Tobi Lütke shared an internal memo on X Monday that stressed the importance of using AI effectively in daily tasks. In fact, he wrote, using AI is now a “fundamental expectation” of Shopify employees.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Europe considers new tariffs that could punish tech companies like Google, Meta, and Apple
item.description: As the European Union looks at how best to respond to Donald Trump’s trade war, officials are considering further escalating things, taking them to a point where it could really hurt America’s Big Tech companies.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: Home services company Thumbtack is thriving even as the real estate market stays slow
item.description: While home sales and remodeling rates remain low, Thumbtack—which connects homeowners with professionals to do work on their houses—says business is booming.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: 9 steps to take to prepare for a quantum future
item.description: Over the past year, vendor after vendor has reached the critical quantum-computing milestone where adding more qubits no longer adds a disproportionately higher amount of errors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: World’s first AI-powered industrial super-humanoid robot
item.description: Mech the super-humanoid robot can lift up to 132 pounds and is designed to tackle stressful and repetitive tasks that often lead to workplace injuries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft employees protest at 50th anniversary party over Israel contract
item.description: A pro-Palestinian protest by Microsoft employees interrupted the company’s 50th anniversary celebration Friday, the latest backlash over the tech industry’s work to supply artificial intelligence technology to the Israeli military.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok deal pulled after Trump tariff announcement
item.description: A finalized TikTok deal was pulled Thursday after President Trump announced massive new tariffs against China, a source familiar with the negotiations told The Hill. Trump was poised to sign an executive order approving a deal that would have seen TikTok’s U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: An AI avatar tried to argue a case before a New York court. The judges weren't having it
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took only seconds for the judges on a New York appeals court to realize that the man addressing them from a video screen — a person about to present an argument in a lawsuit — not only had no law degree, but didn't exist at all.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A cyber call to arms
item.description: Without a call to arms, the United States is dangerously vulnerable today, and still more tomorrow, to increasingly sophisticated and brazen cyberattacks. The post A cyber call to arms first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: AI vs. pro gambler's $1 million March Madness bet coming down to Duke and Houston
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — The $1 million March Madness wager between a pro gambler and an artificial-intelligence site will be decided by the winner of the Duke-Houston game in the Final Four. The Vegas bookies project the gambler, who picked 5 1/2-point favorite Duke, should win.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, PIX 11
